00024 #include "wrappers.h"
00025 
00026 #include "memory.h"
00027 #include "thread.h"
00028 #include "utf8_string.h"
00029 #include "util.h"
00030 #include "vm.h"
00031 
00032 /******************************************************************************
00033  * Type definitions                                                           *
00034  ******************************************************************************/
00035 
00046 struct utf8_string_t {
00047     struct utf8_string_t *next; 
00048     char data[]; 
00049 };
00050 
00052 typedef struct utf8_string_t utf8_string_t;
00053 
00056 struct string_manager_t {
00057     size_t load; 
00058     size_t threshold; 
00059     size_t entries; 
00060     size_t capacity; 
00061     utf8_string_t **buckets; 
00062 };
00063 
00065 typedef struct string_manager_t string_manager_t;
00066 
00067 /******************************************************************************
00068  * Local declarations                                                         *
00069  ******************************************************************************/
00070 
00072 static string_manager_t sm;
00073 
00074 /******************************************************************************
00075  * Local functions prototypes                                                 *
00076  ******************************************************************************/
00077 
00078 static void string_manager_rehash(size_t);
00079 static uint32_t utf8_hash(const char *, size_t);
00080 
00081 /******************************************************************************
00082  * String manager implementation                                              *
00083  ******************************************************************************/
00084 
00090 void string_manager_init(size_t log2cap, size_t load)
00091 {
00092     assert(log2cap != 0);
00093     assert(log2cap < 32);
00094     assert(load != 0);
00095 
00096     sm.capacity = 1 << log2cap;
00097     sm.threshold = 4;
00098     sm.load = load;
00099     sm.entries = 0;
00100 
00101     sm.buckets = gc_malloc(sm.capacity * sizeof(utf8_string_t *));
00102 } // string_manager_init()
00103 
00107 static void string_manager_rehash(size_t capacity)
00108 {
00109     utf8_string_t **buckets;
00110     utf8_string_t *str, *next;
00111     char *data;
00112     size_t len;
00113     uint32_t hash;
00114 
00115     buckets = gc_malloc(capacity * sizeof(utf8_string_t *));
00116 
00117     for (size_t i = 0; i < sm.capacity; i++) {
00118         str = sm.buckets[i];
00119 
00120         while (str != NULL) {
00121             data = str->data;
00122             len = strlen(data);
00123 
00124             /* Mask the hash to extract the bits that fit the current table
00125              * capacity */
00126             hash = utf8_hash(data, len) & (capacity - 1);
00127             next = str->next;
00128             str->next = buckets[hash];
00129             buckets[hash] = str;
00130             str = next;
00131         }
00132     }
00133 
00134     gc_free(sm.buckets);
00135     sm.buckets = buckets;
00136     sm.capacity = capacity;
00137 } // string_manager_rehash()
00138 
00145 bool utf8_check(const char *src)
00146 {
00147     size_t i = 0;
00148 
00149     while (src[i] != '\0') {
00150         switch ((unsigned char) src[i] >> 4) {
00151             case 0x8:
00152             case 0x9:
00153             case 0xA:
00154             case 0xB:
00155                 return false;
00156 
00157             case 0xC:
00158             case 0xD: // 110xxxxx 10xxxxxx
00159                 if ((src[i + 1] & 0xC0) != 0x80) {
00160                     return false;
00161                 }
00162 
00163                 i += 2;
00164                 break;
00165 
00166             case 0xE: // 1110xxxx 10xxxxxx 10xxxxxx
00167                 if ((src[i + 1] & 0xC0) != 0x80) {
00168                     return false;
00169                 }
00170 
00171                 if ((src[i + 2] & 0xC0) != 0x80) {
00172                     return false;
00173                 }
00174 
00175                 i += 3;
00176                 break;
00177 
00178             case 0xF:
00179                 return false;
00180 
00181             default:
00182                 i++;
00183                 break;
00184         }
00185     }
00186 
00187     return true;
00188 } // utf8_check()
00189 
00201 char *utf8_intern(const char *src, size_t len)
00202 {
00203     utf8_string_t *str;
00204     char *empty = "";
00205     uint32_t hash;
00206 
00207     if (len == 0) {
00208         src = empty;
00209     }
00210 
00211     tm_lock(); // Only one thread at a time can intern an UTF-8 string
00212 
00213     // Mask the hash to save the bits that fit the current table capacity
00214     hash = utf8_hash(src, len) & (sm.capacity - 1);
00215     str = sm.buckets[hash];
00216 
00217     while (str != NULL) {
00218         if (strcmp(str->data, src) == 0) {
00219             break;
00220         } else {
00221             str = str->next;
00222         }
00223     }
00224 
00225     if (str == NULL) {
00226         str = gc_palloc(sizeof(utf8_string_t) + len + 1);
00227         memcpy(str->data, src, len);
00228         // No zero termination as the memory has already been cleared
00229         str->next = sm.buckets[hash];
00230         sm.buckets[hash] = str;
00231         sm.entries++;
00232 
00233         /* Since interned UTF8 strings usually belong to a class' constant pool
00234          * they are permanent objects, the manager's hash-table is always grown
00235          * when rehashed and never shrinks */
00236         if (sm.entries > (sm.capacity * sm.load)) {
00237             string_manager_rehash(sm.capacity << 1);
00238         }
00239     }
00240 
00241     tm_unlock();
00242     return str->data;
00243 } // utf8_intern()
00244 
00251 size_t utf8_to_java_length(const char *str)
00252 {
00253     size_t i = 0;
00254     size_t len = 0;
00255 
00256     while (str[i] != 0) {
00257         len++;
00258 
00259         if (str[i] & 0x80) {
00260             if (str[i] & 0x20) { // 1110xxxx 10xxxxxx 10xxxxxx
00261                 i += 3;
00262             } else { // 110xxxxx 10xxxxxx
00263                 i += 2;
00264             }
00265         } else {
00266             i++;
00267         }
00268     }
00269 
00270     return len;
00271 } // utf8_to_java_length()
00272 
00279 void utf8_to_java(uint16_t *dst, const char *src)
00280 {
00281     size_t i = 0;
00282     size_t j = 0;
00283 
00284     while (src[i] != '\0') {
00285         switch ((unsigned char) src[i] >> 4) {
00286             case 0xc:
00287             case 0xd: // 110xxxxx 10xxxxxx
00288                 dst[j] = ((src[i] & 0x1f) << 6) | (src[i + 1] & 0x3f);
00289                 i += 2;
00290                 break;
00291 
00292             case 0xe: // 1110xxxx 10xxxxxx 10xxxxxx
00293                 dst[j] = (((src[i]) & 0xf) << 12)
00294                          | ((src[i + 1] & 0x3f) << 6) | (src[i + 2] & 0x3f);
00295                 i += 3;
00296                 break;
00297 
00298             default:
00299                 dst[j] = src[i];
00300                 i++;
00301                 break;
00302         }
00303 
00304         j++;
00305     }
00306 } // utf8_to_java()
00307 
00313 char *java_to_utf8(const uint16_t *data, size_t length)
00314 {
00315     size_t utf8_length = 1; // At least the nul character is needed
00316     uint16_t jc;
00317     char *str;
00318 
00319     for (size_t i = 0; i < length; i++) {
00320         jc = data[i];
00321 
00322         // FIXME: Add support for surrogates
00323         if ((jc >= 0x0001) && (jc <= 0x007f)) {
00324             utf8_length++;
00325         } else if ((jc == 0) || ((jc >= 0x0080) && (jc <= 0x07ff))) {
00326             utf8_length += 2;
00327         } else if (jc >= 0x0800) {
00328             utf8_length += 3;
00329         }
00330     }
00331 
00332     str = gc_malloc(utf8_length);
00333     str[utf8_length - 1] = 0; // Zero termination
00334 
00335     for (size_t i = 0, j = 0; i < length; i++) {
00336         jc = data[i];
00337 
00338         // FIXME: Add support for surrogates
00339         if ((jc >= 0x0001) && (jc <= 0x007f)) {
00340             str[j++] = jc;
00341         } else if ((jc == 0) || ((jc >= 0x0080) && (jc <= 0x07ff))) {
00342             str[j++] = 0xc0 | ((jc >> 6) & 0x1f);
00343             str[j++] = 0x80 | (jc & 0x3f);
00344         } else if (jc >= 0x0800) {
00345             str[j++] = 0xe0 | (jc >> 12);
00346             str[j++] = 0x80 | ((jc >> 6) & 0x3f);
00347             str[j++] = 0x80 | (jc & 0x3f);
00348         }
00349     }
00350 
00351     return str;
00352 } // java_to_utf8()
00353 
00358 char *utf8_slashify(char *src)
00359 {
00360     size_t len = strlen(src);
00361 
00362     for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
00363         if (src[i] == '.') {
00364             src[i] = '/';
00365         }
00366     }
00367 
00368     return src;
00369 } // utf8_slashify()
00370 
00375 static uint32_t utf8_hash(const char *data, size_t len)
00376 {
00377     uint32_t hash = 5381;
00378 
00379     for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
00380         hash = ((hash << 5) + hash) ^ data[i];
00381     }
00382 
00383     return hash;
00384 } // utf8_hash()
00385 
00386 #ifndef NDEBUG
00387 
00390 size_t string_manager_size( void )
00391 {
00392     utf8_string_t *str;
00393     size_t size;
00394 
00395     size = sizeof(string_manager_t) + sizeof(jword_t);
00396     size += (sizeof(utf8_string_t *) * sm.capacity) + sizeof(jword_t);
00397 
00398     for (size_t i = 0; i < sm.capacity; i++) {
00399         str = sm.buckets[i];
00400 
00401         while (str != NULL) {
00402             size += offsetof(utf8_string_t, data)
00403                     + size_ceil(strlen(str->data) + 1, sizeof(jword_t))
00404                     + sizeof(jword_t);
00405             str = str->next;
00406         }
00407     }
00408 
00409     return size;
00410 } // string_manager_size()
00411 
00414 void string_manager_print( void )
00415 {
00416     utf8_string_t *str;
00417 
00418     printf("sm = \n"
00419            "    load = %zu\n"
00420            "    threshold = %zu\n"
00421            "    entries = %zu\n"
00422            "    capacity = %zu\n"
00423            "    buckets = %p\n",
00424            sm.load, sm.threshold, sm.entries, sm.capacity, (void *) sm.buckets);
00425 
00426     for (size_t i = 0; i < sm.capacity; i++) {
00427         str = sm.buckets[i];
00428 
00429         printf("    buckets[%zu] = \n", i);
00430 
00431         while (str != NULL) {
00432             printf("\t%s\n", str->data);
00433             str = str->next;
00434         }
00435     }
00436 } // print_string_manager()
00437 
00438 #endif // !NDEBUG
